Acton-Boxborough Regional School District

Jacob Fay

Candidate for School Committee Member
Election Day — April 28, 2026
Read My Statement
Candidate Statement

Why I'm Running

Like many families in the AB schools, I've felt the weight of this past year. When reorganization first came onto the table, I had real questions. I listened as families around me worked through uncertainty, frustration, and genuine grief about changes to schools they love. I don't take any of that lightly. Decisions about schools are never just about buildings or budgets. They're about kids, communities, and the kind of place we want to live in together.

Which is exactly why what comes next matters so much: supporting children, families, and AB educators through the transition, protecting classrooms against budget pressures we didn't entirely create and can't fully control, and making five new school communities feel like home to every family in this district.

That's the work. And it's why I'm running.

About Jacob

Background & Experience

I am, first and foremost, a former middle school history teacher. I know what goes on in a classroom. That experience led me to a doctorate from the Harvard Graduate School of Education focused on education policy, including research on what communities actually need to navigate school reorganization well. I've co-written two books on the complexity of educational decision-making, and maintain close connections with leading thinkers in the field across genuine disagreement about what schools should do and how.

As much as I value that intellectual work, what energizes me is being in schools, with the people who run them and the families who depend on them. Three years ago, I left the academy to put my ideas to work as Director of Education at the Constructive Dialogue Institute, a nonpartisan, nonprofit that helps universities and communities build the capacity to work through deep disagreement without losing each other.

I also want to be clear: I have skin in this game. My children are in AB schools right now, navigating this transition alongside every other family. I coach AB youth sports. I've served as President of the Patriots Hill Recreation Club. My investment in this community isn't professional — it's personal, and it's daily.

Priorities

Three Defining Challenges

The next School Committee will face three challenges that will define this district's next chapter. Tap each to learn more.

01
Overseeing AB Forward
+

The immediate task for this Committee is to advance the strategies outlined in the AB Forward Strategic Plan: setting clear policy direction while leaving implementation where it belongs: with the Superintendent and school leadership. As our district transitions to a geographic assignment policy with five elementary schools, the Committee's role is to establish the policy framework, hold administration accountable to it, and ensure transparency and clear communication with the public.

That accountability extends across the whole district. The elementary transition cannot come at the expense of the programs, teachers, and opportunities that make our middle and high schools exceptional, and it is the Committee's job to make sure it doesn't.

02
Stabilizing the Budget
+

The budget is the defining challenge of this Committee's next term — and it will require honesty about what we're up against. Enrollment has declined by roughly 600 students since regionalization, with projections showing another 300 lost over the next eight years. Expenses — driven by contracted salary obligations, rising health insurance costs, and retirement contributions — continue to outpace revenues. State funding formulas haven't kept pace, and federal uncertainty adds further risk. The last override bought time; it did not solve the structural problem.

The Committee's job is to set policy that confronts this honestly: protecting classrooms and direct student services first, scrutinizing every other line, and being transparent with Acton and Boxborough residents about the real tradeoffs ahead. That scrutiny must apply district-wide. The elementary reorganization cannot become a reason to quietly absorb cuts at RJ Grey and ABRHS; the programs, teachers, and opportunities that define those schools deserve the same vigilance.

03
Superintendent Search
+
The next School Committee will select the superintendent who leads this district for the next decade. That is not an overstatement, it is the nature of the role. The right leader, hired through a search that genuinely reflects the priorities of Acton and Boxborough families, can accelerate everything AB Forward is building: a district that is academically excellent, financially stable, and worth the investment this community makes in it. A search that is rushed, opaque, or driven by insider consensus risks squandering that opportunity. The Committee owes this community a process that is transparent, inclusive, and worthy of the decision.

Jacob Fay for Acton-Boxborough Regional School Committee
